evals.expected_output: Findings list identifying: file naming violation (camelCase filename instead of kebab-case), missing try/catch on fetchUserProfile and updateProfile, missing JSDoc on all exported functions, empty catch block in deleteUser, wrong import order (local before third-party). Violations grouped by severity.
expectations: Reports a violation for the filename userProfile.js (not kebab-case)
Reports the empty catch block in deleteUser as a violation
Reports missing JSDoc comments on exported functions
Findings are grouped by severity tier

evals.expected_output: No violations found — date-utils.js fully complies with the standards. File uses kebab-case, constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, async function has try/catch with error logging, and a default export groups the functions.
expectations: Reports no violations (or only nit-level items)
Does not fabricate violations that aren't in the standards
Explicitly confirms the file is compliant

evals.prompt: run a standards check on routes/orders.js
evals.expected_output: Multiple Critical/Major violations: DB queries in route handler (should be in repositories), business logic in route handler (should be in services), route handler has try/catch (should bubble up), raw string thrown instead of Error object, response format doesn't match required shape. Offers to fix after presenting findings.
expectations: Flags DB queries in the route handler as a violation of the architecture standard
Flags the try/catch in the route handler (routes must not have try/catch)
Flags the raw string throw as a violation
Flags the response format not matching { success, data, error }
Offers to fix the violations after presenting findings
